book.title: The Art of War
book.author: Sun Tzu
book.desc: Ancient Chinese military treatise offering timeless wisdom on strategy, leadership and conflict resolution.

quote.text: In the midst of chaos, there is also opportunity.
quote.source: Sun Tzu, The Art of War

book.title: How to Win Friends and Influence People
book.author: Dale Carnegie
book.desc: The classic guide to interpersonal skills that has helped millions build better relationships and succeed in life.

quote.text: You can make more friends in two months by becoming interested in other people than in two years by trying to get people interested in you.
quote.source: Dale Carnegie, How to Win Friends and Influence People

book.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
book.author: Daniel Kahneman
book.desc: Nobel laureate dissects two modes of human thought, revealing systematic errors in our decision-making.

quote.text: We are often overconfident about what we know about the world, and underestimate the role of chance in events.
quote.source: Daniel Kahneman, Thinking, Fast and Slow

book.title: Atomic Habits
book.author: James Clear
book.desc: Proven methods for building good habits and breaking bad ones through tiny, incremental changes.

quote.text: Improve by 1% each day, and in a year you'll be 37 times better. Decline by 1%, and you'll approach zero.

book.title: Sapiens
book.author: Yuval Noah Harari
book.desc: A sweeping narrative of humanity from the Cognitive Revolution to the present, challenging everything we thought we knew.

quote.text: The iron rule of history is that what seems inevitable in hindsight was far from obvious at the time.

book.title: Deep Work
book.author: Cal Newport
book.desc: A guide to rebuilding focus in the age of distraction and producing work of real value.

quote.text: High-quality work produced = Time spent x Intensity of focus.

book.title: The Courage to Be Disliked
book.author: Ichiro Kishimi & Fumitake Koga
book.desc: An introduction to Adlerian psychology through Socratic dialogue, exploring how to find true freedom.

quote.text: True freedom is being disliked by other people.
quote.source: Ichiro Kishimi, The Courage to Be Disliked

book.title: The Almanack of Naval Ravikant
book.author: Eric Jorgenson
book.desc: Collected wisdom from Silicon Valley's philosopher-investor on wealth creation and finding happiness.

quote.text: Play iterated games. All the returns in life — whether in wealth, relationships, or knowledge — come from compound interest.
quote.source: Eric Jorgenson, The Almanack of Naval Ravikant

book.title: Four Thousand Weeks
book.author: Oliver Burkeman
book.desc: A radical rethinking of time management for finite humans — embrace your limits to live a meaningful life.

quote.text: The real measure of any time management technique is whether it helps you neglect the right things.

book.title: The Course of Love
book.author: Alain de Botton
book.desc: A novel that explores what happens after the initial thrill of falling in love — the long, complex journey of sustaining it.

quote.text: Love is a skill, not just an enthusiasm.
quote.source: Alain de Botton, The Course of Love

book.title: The Design of Everyday Things
book.author: Don Norman
book.desc: A seminal exploration of user-centered design and how good design becomes invisible to the user.

quote.text: Good design is actually a lot harder to notice than poor design, in part because good designs fit our needs so well that the design is invisible.
quote.source: Don Norman, The Design of Everyday Things

book.title: Steal Like an Artist
book.author: Austin Kleon
book.desc: A manifesto showing how developing your creative voice means learning from and building on the work you admire.

quote.text: The best way to come up with good ideas is to come up with lots of ideas.
quote.source: Austin Kleon, Steal Like an Artist

book.title: Gödel, Escher, Bach
book.author: Douglas Hofstadter
book.desc: A landmark exploration of consciousness, patterns, and self-reference through music, art, and mathematics.

quote.text: We are strange loops: cycling systems which strange-loop back to themselves in a complex, self-reinforcing way.

book.title: Thinking in Systems: A Primer
book.author: Donella H. Meadows
book.desc: An elegant introduction to systems thinking and how to understand complex interconnected problems.

quote.text: The behavior of a system cannot be known just by knowing the elements of which the system is made.
quote.source: Donella H. Meadows, Thinking in Systems

book.title: Zero to One
book.author: Peter Thiel
book.desc: A guide to building the future by creating something genuinely new rather than copying what exists.

quote.text: The most contrarian thing of all is not to oppose the crowd but to think for yourself.

book.title: Range
book.author: David Epstein
book.desc: Why generalists who embrace diverse experiences and broad knowledge excel at solving novel problems.

quote.text: The ability to make distant connections is what we celebrate as creativity.

book.title: Braiding Sweetgrass
book.author: Robin Wall Kimmerer
book.desc: Indigenous botanist weaves together science, nature writing, and indigenous wisdom to explore our relationship with the living world.

quote.text: We are caretakers, not owners. Our gifts come with the responsibility to reciprocate and care for what sustains us.

book.title: Educated
book.author: Tara Westover
book.desc: A transformative memoir of a woman who leaves an isolated survivalist family to pursue education and self-discovery.

quote.text: I am no longer afraid. I took my education into my own hands.

book.title: Man's Search for Meaning
book.author: Viktor E. Frankl
book.desc: A Holocaust survivor's profound meditation on finding meaning in suffering and how the search for purpose can sustain us through adversity.

quote.text: The last of the human freedoms is to choose one's attitudes in any given set of circumstances.
quote.source: Viktor E. Frankl, Man's Search for Meaning

book.title: The Little Prince
book.author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry
book.desc: A poetic fable about a young prince's journey across distant planets, exploring timeless themes of love, loss, friendship, and what truly matters in life.

quote.text: And now here is my secret, a very simple secret: It is only with the heart that one can see rightly; what is essential is invisible to the eye.
quote.source: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry, The Little Prince

book.title: The Midnight Library
book.author: Matt Haig
book.desc: A woman on the edge of despair discovers a magical library containing all the lives she could have lived, learning that every life has infinite possibility.

quote.text: Between life and death, there is a library. It holds all possible versions of your life.

book.title: The Invention of Nature
book.author: Andrea Wulf
book.desc: A sweeping biography of explorer-scientist Alexander von Humboldt, whose revolutionary vision unified nature as an interconnected whole.

quote.text: Everything is interconnected. The universe is one unified organism, not a collection of separate things.
quote.source: Andrea Wulf, The Invention of Nature

book.title: Humankind
book.author: Rutger Bregman
book.desc: A revolutionary take on human history that challenges the assumption we're selfish by nature, revealing evidence of our natural cooperation and kindness.

quote.text: History is not a catalogue of disasters. It is a story about improbable possibilities.

book.title: Dune
book.author: Frank Herbert
book.desc: An epic tale of political intrigue, prophecy, and survival on the desert planet Arrakis, exploring themes of power, destiny, and human potential.

quote.text: Fear is the mind-killer. Fear is the little-death that brings total obliteration. I will face my fear.

book.title: Bird by Bird
book.author: Anne Lamott
book.desc: A beloved guide to writing and life, offering practical wisdom and spiritual insight on overcoming perfectionism and finding your voice.

quote.text: Writing and reading decrease our sense of isolation. They deepen and widen and expand our sense of life.
